description_long: This is a large, four-engine military transport airplane with a robust, utilitarian design. The fuselage is cylindrical and tapers at the nose and tail, with a high-mounted straight wing configuration and four prominent turboprop engines mounted on the wings. The tail features a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer mounted low on the tail. The landing gear appears to be a tricycle configuration, with the main gear under the wings and a nose gear under the cockpit. The exterior is painted in a muted gray color, typical of military aircraft, and the surface appears smooth and metallic. The cockpit is located at the forward end of the fuselage, with multiple windows providing visibility for the crew.
description: A large, gray, four-engine military transport airplane with high-mounted straight wings, a conventional tail, and a forward cockpit.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the fuselage, high-mounted wings, and four turboprop engines.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the straight wingspan, twin horizontal stabilizers, and the tail's vertical stabilizer.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the engines, wing configuration, and the aircraft's robust body.
description_view_3: Front view emphasizing the nose, cockpit windows, and the symmetrical arrangement of the four engines on the wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Four turboprop engines mounted on the wings.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and low-mounted horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main gear units and a single nose gear.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
